mnemonics.title: Transformation Order Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: T.O.P. = Translate Origin, Perform, Put Back. Remember: To rotate/scale about arbitrary point, first move origin to that point, perform transformation, then move origin back.

mnemonics.title: Matrix Position Memory
mnemonics.content: SHEAR: Scale/Shear/Homog in top-left. E = Everything else: Rotation effects appear in top-left 2×2, Translation in bottom row for 3×3 matrix.

mnemonics.title: Non-Commutative Reminder
mnemonics.content: Think: Rubbing your stomach and patting your head — doing them in different orders gives different results! Transformation matrices are like that too.

acronyms.usage: Add extra dimension (w=1) to enable matrix representation of translation alongside rotation and scaling.
The active transformation matrix in the graphics pipeline that accumulates all applied transformations.
The world coordinate system; pre-multiplication transforms in GCS, affecting all local transformations.
Object's own coordinate system; post-multiplication transforms in LCS, relative to object's current orientation.

visualTips.visualization: Draw 3×3 grid: Top-left 2×2 = rotation/scale (a,b,c,d), Bottom row = translation (l,m), Last column = perspective (p,q), Bottom-right = scale (s)
Think of transformation matrices as functions: M1 × M2 means apply M2 first, then M1 — like f(g(x)), inner applies first!
Picture object at P, move P to origin (slide), spin, slide back — like a potter's wheel centered at different points.

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe: adding ingredients in different orders produces different dishes. Matrix multiplication is like following transformation 'recipes' where order matters.

Local = turning the steering wheel while driving (you rotate in your own frame). Global = the car turning on the road (the road's frame changes your position). Pre-multiplication = global, post = local.
Like UNDO in editors: if you translated by (5,3), inverse translates by (-5,-3). If you rotated 45° clockwise, inverse rotates 45° counter-clockwise.
